runtime_bundle.claim_boundary: this runtime bundle freezes the canonical post-article weighted plugin controller trace above the result-binding contract, host-owned runtime API, and invocation-receipt layer. It keeps plugin selection, export selection, packet encoding, multi-step continuation, retry, typed refusal return, and stop conditions machine-readable while making host execution-only steps explicit and keeping publication, served/public universality, and arbitrary software capability blocked.

validation_rows.detail: plugin selection stays model-owned on every declared controller case.
export selection stays model-owned instead of falling back to runtime defaults.
packet arguments stay model-owned and remain bound to the result-binding contract.
multi-step continuation and stop transitions stay model-owned instead of host-scripted.

claim_boundary: this sandbox report closes the bounded weighted plugin controller trace on the canonical post-article machine identity. It proves that plugin selection, export selection, packet encoding, sequencing, typed-refusal handling, retry, and stop conditions remain model-owned while the host validates and executes declared calls without becoming the planner. It still does not widen plugin authority into publication, trust-tier promotion, served/public universality, or arbitrary public software execution.
